title: Derwentia formosa
taxonomy: Derwentia formosa is a plant species in the family Goodeniaceae. The genus Derwentia is part of the order Asterales. Derwentia formosa is native to Australia and is often found in the eastern regions. It is a member of the angiosperm clade and is further classified into various ranks, including kingdom and phylum. The classification of Derwentia formosa reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
uses: Derwentia formosa is a species of flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in New Zealand. Its medicinal uses include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's properties are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects. Derwentia formosa should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

side_effects: Derwentia formosa, a plant native to Australia, has been reported to cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly when handling its sap or leaves. It may also cause eye irritation or discomfort if the sap comes into contact with the eyes. The sap contains compounds that can be toxic, so it is recommended to handle the plant with care and avoid ingesting it.
